mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generate positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months where industrial production increases, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses a 2.5 percent annual growth threshold, inflation expectations force monetary policy tightening fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPI growth exceeds 2.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal generates negative 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic activity that directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day equity returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces near-term earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.
